I didn't know how to stay
You believe she can see through
Cutting at the silent clay
A relief, beckon deep blue
Fettered in the magnet sun
Eat the gun as it feeds you
Spitting at the oxygen
Once again
Once again
Lord, I no longer believe
Drowned in living waters
Cursed by the love that I received
From my brother's daughter
Like Hephaestion, who died
Alexander's lover
Now my riverbed has dried
Shall I find no other?

other.linePrev: And you could be my brother
other.line: Once again, fall asleep with our backs against each other
stanza: Little Andy, soft in your newborn skin
Only one, little Andy, will you return again?
I believe we can renew
And you could be my brother
Once again, fall asleep with our backs against each other
You believe, I believe too
That you are the river of light who I love
That I sing to in the belly of the empty night

partner.line: And you could be my brother

old.linePrev: Running over the same old ground, what have we found?
old.line: The same old fears, wish you were here
stanza: How I wish, how I wish you were here
We're just two lost souls swimming in a fishbowl, year after year
Running over the same old ground, what have we found?
The same old fears, wish you were here

old.linePrev: Again, I've lost my strength completely, oh be near me
old.line: Tired old mare, with the wind in your hair
old.lineNext: Amethyst and flowers on the table (On the table)
stanza: Somewhere in the desert, there’s a forest
And an acre before us
But I don’t know where to begin
But I don’t know where to begin
Again, I've lost my strength completely, oh be near me
Tired old mare, with the wind in your hair

old.line: Out there in the cold, getting lonely, getting old, can you feel me?
stanza: Hey, you
Out there in the cold, getting lonely, getting old, can you feel me?
Hey, you
Standing in the aisles with itchy feet and fading smiles, can you feel me?
Hey, you, don't help them to bury the light
Don't give in without a fight

old.linePrev: For want of the price of tea and a slice
stanza: Out of the way, it's a busy day
I've got things on my mind
For want of the price of tea and a slice
The old man died

old.linePrev: Ready to walk you through the night
old.line: Old stars filling up my throat
old.lineNext: You gave 'em to me when I was born, now they’re coming out
stanza: Old stars filling up my throat
You gave 'em to me when I was born, now they’re coming out
Laying there on the hospital bed, eyes were narrow, blue and red
You took a draw of breath and said to me

old.linePrev: Ready to walk you through the night
old.line: Old friends, old mothers, dogs and brothers
old.lineNext: There’s only so much letting go you can ask someone to do
stanza: Old friends, old mothers, dogs and brothers
There’s only so much letting go you can ask someone to do
So I keep you by my side, I will not give you to the tide
I'll even walk you in my stride, Marie

okay.line: Go get a good job with more pay, and you're okay
stanza: Money, get away
Go get a good job with more pay, and you're okay
Money, it's a gas
Grab that cash with both hands and make a stash
Okay (Okay, okay, okay)
Just a little pinprick
There'll be no more
But you may feel a little sick
Can you stand up? (Stand up, stand up)
I do believe it's working, good
That'll keep you going through the show
Come on, it's time to go

offhand.linePrev: You fritter and waste the hours
offhand.lineNext: Kicking around on a piece of ground
stanza: Ticking away
The moments that make up a dull day
You fritter and waste the hours
In an offhand way
Kicking around on a piece of ground
In your hometown
Waiting for someone
Or something to show you the way

older.linePrev: The sun is the same in a relative way
stanza: And you run, and you run
To catch up with the sun, but it's sinking
And racing around
To come up behind you again
The sun is the same in a relative way
But you're older
Shorter of breath
And one day closer to death

own.linePrev: Covered in lines, the fossils I find
own.line: Have they no life of their own?
stanza: Are we to speak, first day of the week
Stumbling words at the bar
Beauty blue eyes, my order of fries
Long Island kindness and wine
Beloved of John, I get it all wrong
I read you for some kind of poem
Covered in lines, the fossils I find
Have they no life of their own?

partner.line: I read you for some kind of poem

own.line: Look around and choose your own ground
own.lineNext: For long you live and high you fly
stanza: Breathe, breathe in the air
Don't be afraid to care
Leave, but don't leave me
Look around and choose your own ground

own.line: Out there on your own, sitting naked by the phone, would you touch me?
stanza: Hey, you
Out there on your own, sitting naked by the phone, would you touch me?
Hey, you
With your ear against the wall, waiting for someone to call out, would you touc…
Hey, you, would you help me to carry the stone?
Open your heart, I'm coming home

ordinary.line: And after all, we're only ordinary men
stanza: Us, and them
And after all, we're only ordinary men
Me, and you
God only knows it's not what we would choose to do

obama.linePrev: They give us guns and drugs, call us thugs, make it they promise to fuck with y…
obama.line: No condom, they fuck with you, Obama say, "What it do?"
stanza: Hopped out the Caddy, just got my dick sucked
The little homies called and said, "The enemies done cliqued up"
Oh, yeah? Puto want to squabble with mi barrio?
Oh, yeah? Tell 'em they can run it for the cardio
Oh, yeah? Everything is everything, it's scandalous
Slow motion for the ambulance, the project filled with cameras
The LAPD gamblin', scramblin', football numbers slanderin'
Niggas' names on paper—you snitched all summer
Streets don't fail me now
They tell me it's a new gang in town
From Compton to Congress
Set-trippin' all around
Ain't nothin' new but a flu of new Demo-Crips and Re-Blood-icans
Red state versus a blue state—which one you governin'?
They give us guns and drugs, call us thugs, make it they promise to fuck with y…
No condom, they fuck with you, Obama say, "What it do?"

obama.linePrev: No condom, they fuck with you, Obama say, "What it do?"
stanza: Obama say, "What it do?"
Obama say, "What it do?"
Obama say, "What it do?"

oregon.line: I’m bright as the Oregon breeze
stanza: I’m light as a feather
I’m bright as the Oregon breeze
My black shroud
Frightened by my feelings
I only wanna be a relief

offend.line: I am a man with a heart that offends
offend.lineNext: With its lonely and greedy demands

I am a man with a heart that offends
